title: lilium pardalinum subsp. shastense
intro: Lilium pardalinum subsp. shastense, also known as Shasta lily, is a rare and endangered subspecies of the tiger lily, native to the mountains of California and Oregon. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, making it a potential remedy for wound healing, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and even exhibit antitumor activity. Herbal preparations of the Shasta lily include dried flowers, rhizomes, and bulbs, which can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes.
